title: 10 Ontario Savings Bonds
body_text:
10	Ontario Savings Bonds
10.1	Introduction to Ontario Savings Bonds
Ontario Savings Bonds are issued by the province of Ontario and are only sold to residents of Ontario. The Province of Ontario backs the principal and interest of Ontario Savings Bonds. For procedures on how to process and redeem Ontario Savings Bonds, refer to the Ontario Savings Bonds website. See Section 1.3, Contacts and Websites.
10.2	Preparing the Deposit for Central 1
Credit unions process and transmit Ontario Savings Bonds on Branch Capture. It is, therefore, not necessary to make a separate record of these items. Images are available on Branch Capture for 180 days and on ORS for 7 years. For more information, see the Deposit Processing Part. Ontario Savings Bonds are deposited separately from Canada Savings Bonds and from other Canadian dollar deposit items. Ontario Savings Bonds must be part of a separate Canadian dollar deposit coded â03â or the deposit wonât be processed. For Branch Capture, separate Ontario Savings Bonds into their own bundle and process them as CAD deposits. Do not include Ontario Savings bonds with other Canadian items or with certificate-style (MICR-encoded) Canada Savings and Canada Premium Bonds.
10.3	Settlement and Adjustments for Ontario Savings Bonds
Central 1 will settle daily for redeemed bonds through the credit unionâs Central 1 Canadian Dollar current account. Central 1 settles for the amount in the redemption value box.
If the incorrect amount is written in the redemption value box, the processing financial institution will return the bond as an unqualified item. The credit union must rescan the corrected item.
END
